title: Reserved Keys on Microsoft Windows
text: Reserved Keys on Microsoft Windows It is possible to redefine the behavior of keys by editing the MSWINX keyboard translation table or using your own customized version. Such changes will affect the behavior of Unifields, but may be ignored by widgets. For example: In a multi-line edit box, Page Up , Page Down , Up , and Down arrows are handled by the widget and any redefinition in the translation table is ignored. In a single-line edit box, the Left and Right arrow keys are also handled by the widget. The following keys are reserved by Microsoft Windows and should not be redefined in Uniface: Alt Alt+Escape Alt+F4 Alt+Hyphen Alt+Insert Alt+PrintScreen Alt+Spacebar Alt+Tab Control+Escape Control+F4 Control+F6 Control+Shift+F6 Control+Insert Control+Tab Delete F10 Insert Home End Page Up Page Dn Control+Alt+Delete Control+Alt+Shift+Delete PrintScreen Shift+Alt+Tab Shift+Delete Shift+Insert Shift+Home Shift+End Shift+Page Up Shift+Page Dn Related Links Keyboard Support on Microsoft Windows Many Uniface applications, including the Uniface IDE, run on Microsoft Windows platforms. Uniface provides the MSWINX keyboard translation table(KTT) as default to handle keyboard input on this platform. This translation table follows Windows conventions for navigation, and cut, copy, and paste actions. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
